rules.title: Repo descriptor present
rules.lesson: C39 — the repo declares itself; applicability, scrutiny, and posture consume a schema-validated descriptor, not per-rule tree-sniffing that misfires and can't express intent
rules.rationale: type/lifecycle/maturity/workflow cannot be reliably inferred from the tree. A minimal baseline.repo.json is the one stored intent every derivation hangs off; absent, the checker falls back to fragile auto-detection. Presence stays a transitional WARN — adoption is invited, not forced. VALIDITY is DESC-02's (M7c split, the FLOW-02/03 presence/content divide): a repo that never declared loses features, but a repo whose declaration is broken loses enforcement it thinks it has — different failure classes, different severities.
rules.fix: Add baseline.repo.json at the repo root: copy templates/baseline.repo.json or a config-presets/*.repo.json posture preset, then set type, lifecycle, maturity, workflow, and anchoring.

rules.title: A present descriptor is schema-valid
rules.lesson: M7b panel filing — ten workflow-gated blockers hang off descriptor validity: an invalid descriptor turns the posture OFF (every lane/divergence blocker silently SKIPs) while the run stays green-looking, which is exactly the silent-demotion class change control exists to prevent
rules.rationale: Deterministic, so blocker is lawful: baseline.repo.json exists and fails schema validation. The engine already answers invalidity with 'workflow contract off' SKIPs on every gated rule — this rule makes that collapse LOUD instead of a warn beside a wall of skips. Absence is DESC-01's warn (no overlap — the FLOW-02/03 divide: presence and content are different findings). Contexts exclude admit deliberately: admit reads the TARGET ref's descriptor (FS1), and a blocker here would hostage the very fix-PR that repairs an invalid main — detection (check/reconcile files the issue) is the honest guarantee, DESC-03 remains the change gate.
rules.fix: Fix the named schema errors, or re-copy a config-presets/*.repo.json preset and re-fill the identity fields. Coming from early V2: drop the retired owner key — MIGRATION.md walks the exit.

rules.title: A descriptor change carries its judgment in the same range
rules.lesson: CF4/FS1 — the descriptor is V2's largest stored intent and a PR must not weaken the posture that judges it; the judgment saying WHY belongs in the same range, not a follow-up promise
rules.rationale: Deterministic, so blocker is lawful: baseline.repo.json appears in the admitted range's diff AND no schema-valid, unexpired judgment whose subject is exactly 'baseline.repo.json' (ONE spelling — the filename, the one constant the tool owns) was added in the same range. The weakening classification (schema x-strictness ladders + gate-consumed set-rules, src/derive/posture.mjs) rides the finding text — M7's per-axis policy seam, not this verdict's fork. The run's own posture comes from the TARGET ref (FS1), so the judged-by descriptor is never the branch's.
rules.fix: In the SAME PR as the descriptor change: baseline jdg new --kind deviation --subject "baseline.repo.json" --reason "why the posture changed" --review-by <date>.
